account.deleteAccountTipsTitle1: Please make it clear that your account is not really deleted, but is placed in a state where it is deleted and cannot be recovered. The following operations will be performed:
account.deleteAccountTips1: Please make it clear that your account is not really deleted, but is placed in a deactivated and unrecoverable state. The following operations will be performed:
account.deleteAccountTipsList2: Your account will have all permissions removed and will be prohibited from logging in. It cannot be retrieved through your email address, which can be used to register another account;
account.deleteAccountTipsList3: Private account information will be removed, but publicly posted content will remain;
account.deleteAccountTipsList4: Game prizes, data, etc. bound to the account will be retained, but information cannot be provided to prove that it is yours.
account.deleteAccountTips2:

If you're really sure you want to deactivate your account, please confirm by entering your full username below:
account.deleteAccountTips3: Please note that due to the deletion of identity verification-related private information, even if you contact management/operation and maintenance personnel, you cannot recover the deactivated account, so please think twice before doing so.

permission.basicIntro:
All registered users have basic permissions. Only users with this permission can log in and use platform functions. 
Users without this permission are equivalent to having their accounts frozen. Generally speaking, this item will be missing from the account only when the user deactivates the account.
permission.verifiedIntro:
The basic permissions that users have after passing email verification. If the platform does not configure or turn off the email verification function, users will obtain this permission immediately after registration. 
With this permission, you can have normal access to all user functions of the platform, such as normal participation in competitions, use of the driving range, etc. 
Adding verification permissions mainly prevents resource consumption attacks.
permission.statisticsIntro:
You can view platform statistics and detailed statistics for each game. 
With this permission, you can view some privacy-related information such as the organization to which the user belongs, but you do not have write permission.
permission.calendarIntro: Competition information can be published in the competition calendar at the bottom of the homepage, and events within and outside the platform can be written here.
permission.publishIntro:
Public content is allowed to be published on the platform, such as announcements, problem solutions, knowledge bases, etc. 
Users with this permission do not go through the audit system when posting content.
permission.auditIntro:
Content posted by users can be audited. 
The platform has a built-in lightweight content security audit system, and content posted by users who have not passed the audit will not be displayed by default. 
Administrators with audit permissions can manually audit this content and decide whether to publicly display/block it.
permission.organizeIntro: It is allowed to hold competitions, maintain practice fields, publish questions, etc., and can access and control most platform resources, as well as view data statistics.
permission.devopsIntro: Allows access to and operation of sensitive server resources and provides a web server asset control panel.

tutorial.welcome: Welcome to the world of CTF!
tutorial.skip: I am a CTF veteran, I don’t need your teaching.
tutorial.whatIsCTF: is generally translated as Capture the Flag in Chinese. In the field of network security, it refers to a form of technical competition among network security technicians.
tutorial.whatIsF12:
Participating in CTF events usually requires you to have a deep understanding of computers. If you don't have one - it doesn't matter, you can start here! 
Learning CTF may be different from other subjects. There is no textbook and no teacher to follow step by step. 
But don’t be afraid, search engines and various forum materials are your best learning tools!
tutorial.tryF12Flag:
There is a flag on the webpage you are looking at now. Try using the developer tools to select the spinning thing on the left! 
Don’t know what developer tools are? 
You can first use a search engine to search for "browser developer tools", and then try to open your browser developer tools (the shortcut key for some browsers is F12, and some are Ctrl Shift I), and look for this 
Is there any hidden content on the page?
tutorial.inputFlagAndNext: Enter the Flag string you found here in the format of flag{...}. If it is correct, the next button on the right will light up.
tutorial.whatIsF12Title:
So how to learn CTF? 
How to solve the challenge? 
Let me see!
tutorial.futherLearning1:
When you first meet CTF, there are generally five major learning directions, namely software reverse engineering (Reverse), binary vulnerability audit (Pwn), Web penetration audit and testing (Web), modern cryptography (Crypto), and miscellaneous (Misc) ( 
Encoding, steganography, forensics and traffic analysis). In future studies, emerging technical directions and more detailed technical categories such as domain penetration, blockchain security, etc. will be added. 
But these are all things for later, you can start from the document below!
tutorial.platformInfoTitle: Next let’s see what’s available on this platform!
tutorial.platformInfoWiki:
Here is the knowledge base! 
We'll be posting about CTF here! 
It performs the same function as the CTF Wiki, and all CTF-related knowledge can be consulted here. 
If there is any missing content here, please give us feedback. During the learning process, it is also recommended to consult the previously recommended CTF knowledge website and search engine.
tutorial.platformInfoPlatform:
This is the playground! 
The practice field contains many, many questions covering various CTF tracks. 
After all competitions held on this platform are archived, you can come to the practice field to continue to reproduce the challenges. 
At the same time, the training ground will also release some practice challenges from time to time for everyone to learn.
tutorial.platformInfoGames:
Here is the event list! 
You can participate in various games held on this platform here!
tutorial.platformInfoAnnouncements:
Here is the platform announcement! 
Some notices will be released, etc. Special notes about the competition, important events, and daily platform maintenance information will be here, so stay tuned!
tutorial.theEnd: Okay, that’s the brief introduction to CTF, but we still have a long, long road to learn... The rest of the time is entirely up to you!
tutorial.doNotProfit: CTF is for fun not for profit. I hope you can still maintain your true heart and love for technology after many years of digging in the field of cyber security.
